item.title: Trump says he will raise tariff on European vehicles to 25%
item.description: Levies on cars and trucks exported to US will be imposed ‘next week’ unless EU companies manufacture at American plants

item.title: Palantir’s latest hit softwear: a $239 chore coat
item.description: Who cares about taste when billions of dollars are being bandied about?

item.title: Oil market one month from crunch point as global stockpiles dwindle
item.description: Traders warn of further jump in prices and ‘huge pain’ as economies cut consumption

item.title: Superdry co-founder found guilty of rape
item.description: James Holder raped a woman in her house after a night out, jury found after criminal trial

item.title: Big airline bosses’ confidence should trouble their investors
item.description: Carriers do not seem to be baking in slowing demand

item.title: Apple credits ‘most popular’ ever iPhone for booming sales
item.description: John Ternus pledges to continue ‘deliberateness and discipline’ in first comments since being named next CEO

item.title: Pentagon signs new military AI deals with Nvidia, Microsoft and Amazon
item.description: New contracts with tech companies come after clash with Anthropic over Claude use

item.title: Governments start daring to say the energy F-words
item.description: First global conference on the transition from fossil fuels speeds the erosion of a needless taboo

item.title: How to future-proof your home from energy shocks
item.description: As war in the Middle East sends household bills soaring, more people are considering defensive tactics to mitigate volatile fossil-fuel prices in the long term

item.title: Exxon and Chevron defy Trump pressure to boost oil production
item.description: US supermajors stick to prewar strategies despite White House plea for more drilling to curb soaring petrol prices

item.title: Private credit group Ares draws nearly $20bn from investors
item.description: Real estate and infrastructure commitments help offset weakness in group’s core business

item.title: Does size matter?
item.description: It turns out that yes, yes it does. And the SEC is involved.

item.title: Liberia threatened with funding loss if it fails to sell carbon credits
item.description: Rainforest nations face intense pressure to sell credits to airlines and wealthier countries

item.title: How robotaxis will reshape the ride-hailing market
item.description: Technological advances have propelled self-driving cars from small-scale testing to rapid global expansion

item.title: NatWest profits hit £2bn as bank benefits from slowing pace of rate cuts
item.description: UK lender expects to generate more than £17bn in income for the year

item.title: Tata’s owner faces forced listing after rule change and boardroom splits
item.description: India’s regulatory shift means conglomerate may struggle to avoid mandatory IPO for holding company

item.title: UK house price rise defies economic impact of Iran war
item.description: Unexpected increase in April supported by relative strength in household finances

item.title: South East Water’s management should be sacked, MPs say
item.description: Shareholders in utility group should also ‘take share of blame’ for failures, says parliamentary report

item.title: Shares in Japan’s Toto soar on AI-related pivot
item.description: Toilet maker unveils plans to boost output of semiconductor components

item.title: FirstFT: Trump’s deepening political troubles
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Israel supplies UAE with laser systems and the quest for Earth 2.0

item.title: Todd Boehly’s insurance empire
item.description: The billionaire investor’s insurer is the biggest user of an unusual investment structure being targeted by US regulators

item.title: Trump vs Kimmel: inside Disney chief Josh D’Amaro’s baptism of fire
item.description: Studio’s boss has run into free speech crisis just weeks into his job

item.title: WPP boss faces pay battle after opposition from proxy shareholder groups
item.description: Investors told to vote against proposed £11mn pay deal for chief executive Cindy Rose next week

item.title: TK Elevator sale shows private equity’s elevator pitch still works
item.description: The only way is up for Cinven and Advent after their deal with Finnish lift maker Kone

item.title: The quest for Earth 2.0
item.description: From the Artemis II Moon mission to investigations of far-flung planets, a new ‘golden age’ of space exploration has arrived

item.title: How bots could help revive democracy
item.description: New AI tools encouraging deliberation could promote consensus and reduce polarisation in politics

item.title: Diageo’s Dave Lewis bets big on canned cocktails
item.description: Drinks maker’s new focus on fast-growing ‘ready to drink’ beverages marks shift in strategy

item.title: Greg Abel’s first test: winning over Warren Buffett’s Berkshire faithful
item.description: Thousands of shareholders in Omaha this weekend will be eager to hear new chief’s vision for the conglomerate

item.title: Iran war diverts more ships from Suez to long route round Africa
item.description: Despite higher costs, shipping companies opt for safer Asia-Europe freight passages via Cape of Good Hope

item.title: PwC will drop cover for weight-loss drugs from employee health plans
item.description: Big Four accountancy balks at high cost of medication it says has become ‘cultural phenomenon’
